text.title: fine-tuning-openai-deployment Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for fine-tuning-openai-deployment as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoding the fine-tuned model ID in application source code.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Model IDs change on every retrain. Hardcoded IDs require a code deploy to update, coupling the retraining pipeline to the application release cycle. Store model IDs in config files or environment variables, updated by the pipeline on successful completion.

failure-mode.description: Routing 100% of production traffic immediately to a new fine-tuned model.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Even a model that passes eval can fail on real production traffic due to distribution differences. Gradual rollout (10% → 50% → 100%) limits blast radius and provides real traffic signals for quality monitoring before full deployment.

failure-mode.description: Not tracking which training data produced which model version.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without version tracking, debugging quality regressions is impossible. When a model degrades, you need to know what changed: training data, hyperparameters, or base model version. Document model ID + training data hash + eval results in version control on every retrain.
